data.narrative:
Mammoth Biosciences is a Series D biotech company founded by Jennifer Doudna and co-founders, having raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private biotech companies at Series C–E stages and 3 public Biotech & Health peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), though public comps remain limited in direct applicability due to the private nature of revenue disclosure.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $600M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, implying $900M. Peer-Median analysis references five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—Abridge ($5.3B, Series E), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B, Series C), Insitro ($2.4B, Series C), Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B, Series C), and New Limit ($810M, Series C)—yielding a median valuation of $2.4B and a range of $810M–$5.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring was not applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed; the median public sector P/S multiple of 6.1x cannot be deployed without this metric.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $765M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median estimate of $1.6B. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and near-term clinical or commercial milestones that would inform stage progression velocity, as well as the high dispersion among peer valuations reflecting varied technology focus and development maturity within the biotech frontier sector.
